import random

artists_listeners = {
    'Lil Baby': 58738173,
    'Roddy Ricch': 34447081,
    'DaBaby': 29659518,
    'Polo G': 27204041,
    'NLE Choppa': 11423582,
    'Lil Tjay': 16873693,
    'Lil Durk': 19827615,
    'Megan Thee Stallion': 26685725,
    'Pop Smoke': 32169881,
    'Lizzo': 9236657,
    'Migos': 23242076,
    'Doja Cat': 33556496,
    'Tyler, The Creator': 11459242,
    'Saweetie': 10217413,
    'Cordae': 4026278,
    'Juice WRLD': 42092985,
    'Travis Scott': 52627586,
    'Post Malone': 65466387,
    'Drake': 71588843,
    'Kendrick Lamar': 33841249,
    'J. Cole': 38726509,
    'Kanye West': 29204328,
    'Lil Nas X': 23824455,
    '21 Savage': 26764030,
    'Lil Uzi Vert': 40941950,
}



keys = list(artists_listeners.keys())
score = 0

while True:
    # start a new game
    first_artist = random.choice(keys)
    second_artist = random.choice(keys)
    while first_artist == second_artist:
        second_artist = random.choice(keys)

    # ask the user to guess which artist has more monthly Spotify listeners
    while True:
   guess = input(f"\nWhich artist has more monthly Spotify listeners - {first_artist.title()} or {second_artist.title()}? ")
    guess_lower = guess.lower()
    if guess_lower == 'quit':
        print("Thanks for playing!")
        quit()
    elif guess_lower not in ['1', '2', first_artist.lower(), second_artist.lower()]:
        print("Invalid input. Please enter the number or name of one of the two artists or 'quit' to end the game.")
    else:
        break

    # map the user's input to the corresponding artist
   if guess_lower in ['1', first_artist.lower()]:
    selected_artist = first_artist
    other_artist = second_artist
    elif guess_lower == '2':
        selected_artist = keys[1]
        other_artist = keys[0]
    elif guess_lower == keys[0].lower():
        selected_artist = keys[0]
        other_artist = keys[1]
    else:
    selected_artist = second_artist
    other_artist = first_artist

    # check the user's guess and update the score
    if artists_listeners[selected_artist] > artists_listeners[other_artist]:
        print(f"You guessed correctly! {selected_artist.title()} had {int(artists_listeners[selected_artist] / 1e6):,}M monthly Spotify listeners and {other_artist.title()} has {int(artists_listeners[other_artist] / 1e6):,}M monthly Spotify listeners.")
        score += 1
    else:
        print(f"You guessed incorrectly. {other_artist.title()} had {int(artists_listeners[other_artist] / 1e6):,}M monthly Spotify listeners and {selected_artist.title()} has {int(artists_listeners[selected_artist] / 1e6):,}M monthly Spotify listeners.")
        play_again = input("Do you want to play again? (Press enter to continue playing or type 'quit' to end the game) ")
        if play_again.lower() == 'quit':
            print("Thanks for playing!")
            break
        else:
            continue
    
    # display the current score and wait for the user to continue playing
    print(f"Your score is {score}.")

Your code looks good. However, I found a few indentation and syntax errors in your code that I have corrected. Here's the corrected code:

```
import random

artists_listeners = {
    'Lil Baby': 58738173,
    'Roddy Ricch': 34447081,
    'DaBaby': 29659518,
    'Polo G': 27204041,
    'NLE Choppa': 11423582,
    'Lil Tjay': 16873693,
    'Lil Durk': 19827615,
    'Megan Thee Stallion': 26685725,
    'Pop Smoke': 32169881,
    'Lizzo': 9236657,
    'Migos': 23242076,
    'Doja Cat': 33556496,
    'Tyler, The Creator': 11459242,
    'Saweetie': 10217413,
    'Cordae': 4026278,
    'Juice WRLD': 42092985,
    'Travis Scott': 52627586,
    'Post Malone': 65466387,
    'Drake': 71588843,
    'Kendrick Lamar': 33841249,
    'J. Cole': 38726509,
    'Kanye West': 29204328,
    'Lil Nas X': 23824455,
    '21 Savage': 26764030,
    'Lil Uzi Vert': 40941950,
}

keys = list(artists_listeners.keys())
score = 0

while True:
    # start a new game
    first_artist = random.choice(keys)
    second_artist = random.choice(keys)
    while first_artist == second_artist:
        second_artist = random.choice(keys)

    # ask the user to guess which artist has more monthly Spotify listeners
    while True:
        guess = input(f"\nWhich artist has more monthly Spotify listeners - {first_artist.title()} or {second_artist.title()}? ")
        guess_lower = guess.lower()
        if guess_lower == 'quit':
            print("Thanks for playing!")
            quit()
        elif guess_lower not in ['1', '2', first_artist.lower(), second_artist.lower()]:
            print("Invalid input. Please enter the number or name of one of the two artists or 'quit' to end the game.")
        else:
            break

    # map the user's input to the corresponding artist
    if guess_lower in ['1', first_artist.lower()]:
        selected_artist = first_artist
        other_artist = second_artist
    elif guess_lower == '2':
        selected_artist = keys[1]
        other_artist = keys[0]
    elif guess_lower == keys[0].lower():
        selected_artist = keys[0]
        other_artist = keys[1]
    else:
        selected_artist = second_artist
        other_artist = first_artist

    # check the user's guess and update the score
    if artists_listeners[selected_artist] > artists_listeners[other_artist]:
        print(f"You guessed correctly! {selected_artist.title()} had {int(artists_listeners[selected_artist] / 1e6):,}M monthly Spotify listeners and {other_artist.title()} has {int(artists_listeners[other_artist] / 1e6):,}M monthly Spotify listeners.")
        score += 1
    else:
        print(f"You guessed incorrectly. {other_artist.title()} had {int(artists_listeners[other_artist] / 1e6):,}M monthly Spotify listeners and {selected_artist.title()} has {int(artists_listeners[selected_artist] / 1e6):,}M monthly Spotify listeners.")
        play_again = input("Do you want to play again? (Press enter to continue playing or type 'quit' to end the game) ")
        if play_again.lower() == 'quit':
            print("Thanks for playing!")
            break
        else:
            continue

    # display the current score and wait for the user to continue playing
    print(f"Your score is {score}.")
```

Feel free to ask any questions if you have any.
it doesnt run when i open the file

Since the code block I provided above was written in plaintext format with no file formatting, you can copy and paste the corrected code in a new Python file, save it with a name and a ".py" extension (e.g., "my_script.py"), and then run it. Here are the steps:

1. Copy and paste the corrected code into a new Python file (e.g., using a text editor).
2. Save the file with a meaningful name and a ".py" extension (e.g., my_script.py).
3. Open a command prompt or terminal window.
4. Navigate to the directory where the file is saved.
5. Run the following command to execute the file:

```
python my_script.py
```

Note that you should have Python installed on your system first in order to run this command.

Also, double-check if the Python file is saved with a .py extension and the name of the file does not contain any spaces or special characters. It is also a good idea to check if you have installed Python and configured your environment variables correctly.
u just double click on the file, the file is correctly saved

When you double-click on a Python file, it launches the file with the default program associated with files with the ".py" extension in your system. By default, most systems associate Python files with the Python interpreter used to install Python on the system.

If nothing happens when you double-click on the file, it's possible that the file association for .py files is not working as expected on your system or the default program is not set correctly.

One way to run the Python script is to open a terminal or command prompt, navigate to the directory where your Python file is stored, and run the file using the following command:

```
python filename.py
```

This will run the Python interpreter and execute your Python code in the file. Make sure to replace "filename" with the actual name of your Python file.

If you encounter any error or issue, please let me know the message and I'll do my best to help you resolve it.

it says : [Errno 2] No such file or directory

The error message "No such file or directory" usually means that the command prompt or terminal is unable to find the file that you want to run. This typically happens if you're running the command from a different directory than where the file is actually located.

To resolve this, you need to navigate to the directory where the Python file is stored in the command prompt or terminal. You can do this by using the `cd` command followed by the path to the directory.

For example, if your Python file is saved in the directory C:\Users\elias\Documents\Code\Python - Freegpt\, you can navigate to the directory using the following command:

```
cd C:\Users\elias\Documents\Code\Python - Freegpt\
```

Once you have navigated to the correct directory, you can run the Python file by entering:

```
python fe2.py
```

This should correctly execute the Python script without any issues.
